name: Gertrude Stein
abstract: Gertrude Stein (February 3, 1874 – July 27, 1946) was an American novelist, poet, playwright, and art collector. Born in Allegheny, Pennsylvania (now part of Pittsburgh), and raised in Oakland, California, Stein moved to Paris in 1903, and made France her home for the remainder of her life.

works_created.t: Doctor Faustus Lights the Lights, A Play

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Clarke
about_quotes.evidence: And Gertrude Stein and Alice B. Toklas were there, and they would say, 'How do you find time to do all this?'
